title: Cloud Storage: Qwik Start – CLI/SDK & Console
aim: To create Cloud Storage buckets, upload/download objects, manage folders, and control access using gsutil CLI and the Google Cloud Console.

steps.title: Part A: Using CLI/SDK (gsutil)
steps.description: Perform storage operations using the Cloud Shell.

steps.title: Create a Bucket
steps.description: Use 'gsutil mb' to create a bucket. Note: bucket names must be globally unique.

steps.title: Upload and Manage Objects
steps.description: Use 'gsutil cp' to upload/download and 'gsutil ls' to list contents.

steps.title: Public Access Control
steps.description: Use 'gsutil acl ch' to make an object public or remove public access.

steps.title: Part B: Using Google Cloud Console
steps.description: Perform same operations via the GUI.

steps.title: GUI Operations
steps.description: Go to Cloud Storage > Buckets. Click Create. Use 'Upload Files' button and 'Permissions' tab for access control.

commands.purpose: Create a new Cloud Storage bucket
Upload a file to the bucket

viva.question: Difference between object and bucket?
viva.answer: A bucket is a container for data (like a folder/drive), and an object is the individual file stored within it.

viva.question: Why bucket name must be unique?
viva.answer: Bucket names are used in the global DNS namespace (e.g., storage.googleapis.com/bucket-name), so they must be unique across all Google Cloud users.

notes: gsutil is the CLI tool for Cloud Storage. 'gs://' is the URI prefix for GCS buckets.
